DEMURRER
Off Calendar May-12-2003 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Diane E. Baylor, Esq. of HS LAW GROUP 408/790-5320, for defendent San Jose Unified School District, demurring party
David Blatte, Esq., 510/548-7382, for plaintiffs In Defense of Animals, Action for Animals, Peggy Hilden on behalf of Colin Hilden, her minor child, opposing the motion
Nancy Huneke, Esq. of STUBBS & LEONE 925/974-8600, also for defendents San Francisco Unified School District, et al.
Peter Sturges, Esq. of MILLER BROWN 310/373-6857, for defendant Vallejo City Unified School District

RULING:
* A R G U E D and the Court adopted its tentative ruling -- DEFENDANT SAN JOSE UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT'S DEMURRER -- Re: c/a 1 - Sustained without leave re: non-profit not a taxpayer and individual not a taxpayer in defendant's school district. Re: c/a 2 (Profession Code Section 17200) - Sustained without leave; defendant is a government entity, not covered by 17200. Re: c/a 3 - Sustained without leave to amend, no claim stated. Re: c/a 4 - Sustained without leave to amend, no justiciable controversy as to moving party defendant.
** Defendant Vallejo City Unified School District's request for a joinder to Defendant San Jose School District's demurrer was stipulated by plaintiff and granted by the Court. (DAG/301/EH) Reporter: Joe Vickstein, CSR 4780
